summary:
The patient likely has either periapical osteofibroses or an undiagnosed condition causing similar symptoms; however, definitive diagnosis requires additional investigation including histopathology for certainty regarding nature of lesion(s).
The presence of viable pulps suggests non-infectious etiology but does not rule out inflammatory causes requiring differential consideration until confirmed through biopsy results. To clarify exact cause necessitates ruling out various pathologies via detailed examination and possibly biopsies. ```plaintext
Radiologic features indicate possible periapical osteofibroses rather than typical granuloma formation since affected tooth roots remain vital. Further diagnostic steps should include tissue sampling to confirm the precise pathology underlying these changes.
```
